threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'corner shop' instead of 'the offie'—everyone’s giving you funny looks at the deli.
Caught a night bus home and overheard two blokes arguing about which 90s sitcom had the best opening theme. Classic Bromley banter.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a saxophonist jamming outside my window at 2 AM while someone yelled 'Berliner Luft!' It felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that doubles as an art gallery, serving up döner with a side of contemporary installations.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to avoid the 14-Mission during rush hour, especially when BART's acting up. It's a game of survival on wheels.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Spotted a guy in the back of the 14 at 16th who was warming up a burrito on the heater. Muni's real-life survival guide.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just waltz into a bar and order tapas. Here, you need to know the secret menu for the good stuff.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by the bewildered look when they first try to order a 'café con leche' and end up with a 'café solo' instead.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a ‘cabbie’ instead of just ‘taxi’. The locals know it’s all about the black cabs, mate.
Caught the N3 last night and the driver was blasting 90s R&B. One guy started a mini dance party in the back, but the rest just rolled their eyes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids playing footie while their parents debated the latest BVG strike over coffee at the corner café.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a döner shop that opens at 3 AM, serving fresh bread and the best late-night chats with locals. No other city gets this.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local when they’re the only one to instinctively brace for a jolt on the BART platform before the train even arrives.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 bus yesterday, and a guy serenaded us with a sax solo. Only in San Francisco would a commute come with an unexpected concert.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every square in Gràcia is just a photo op, but locals know they’re vibrant community spaces where the real conversations happen.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newcomer by their enthusiasm for trying to pronounce 'xampanyet'—it's almost a rite of passage here in Gràcia.
